description_long: This irregularly shaped stone features a rugged, angular form with a mix of sharp and blunt edges. Its surface displays a blend of pale gray, off-white, and muted yellowish-brown tones, with some areas appearing more weathered and rough than others. The stone has a natural, unpolished texture, with visible mineral inclusions and subtle color gradations that suggest a sedimentary or metamorphic origin. One side is notably rougher and darker, possibly indicating exposure to soil or organic material, while the other faces are lighter and more uniform. The stone's size and mass make it a substantial obstacle, suitable for use as a barrier or natural urban feature. Its overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to the elements, with minor signs of surface weathering and discoloration.
description: A rugged, angular stone with pale gray and yellowish-brown tones, featuring a rough, weathered surface and visible mineral inclusions.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, flat face with a mix of white and brownish-gray coloration and a visible rough patch on one side.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone appears more elongated, with a prominent ridge and a blend of gray and yellow hues.
description_view_2: This side shows a smoother, lighter gray surface with subtle yellow mottling and fewer dark inclusions.
description_view_3: The view reveals a rough, dark brown area contrasting with lighter stone, emphasizing the stone's irregular, jagged profile.
attribute_location: urban park edge or roadside embankment
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a natural barrier
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and mineral veining with irregular, natural color transitions; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with minor discoloration and surface pitting, indicating moderate exposure to weather and soil.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with soil, gravel, or other natural stones in landscaped areas.
